_comment: Per-chapter Scripture Gate. To pass a chapter the pilgrim must answer its key verse correctly; a wrong choice lets them choose again. answer = index of the correct option. Bilingual (zh primary, en in parentheses on each option).

city_of_destruction.prompt: 传道者催你逃离将倾之城。挪亚因着什么预备了方舟,使全家得救?
city_of_destruction.prompt_en: Evangelist urges you to flee the doomed city. By what did Noah build the ark and save his household?
options: 因着信 (By faith)
因着运气 (By luck)
因着财富 (By wealth)
因着聪明 (By cleverness)
city_of_destruction.correct: 「挪亚因着信……预备了一只方舟,使他全家得救。」(来 11:7)
city_of_destruction.wrong: 再想想——救恩的根基不是别的,正是信。

wilderness_road.prompt: 旧城的声音仍在耳边。你当怎样奔那摆在前头的路程?
wilderness_road.prompt_en: The old city's voices still follow. How should you run the race set before you?
options: 存心忍耐,仰望耶稣 (With perseverance, eyes on Jesus)
凭自己的血气 (In my own strength)
回头再看一眼 (By looking back)
走最易走的路 (By the easiest road)
wilderness_road.correct: 「存心忍耐,奔那摆在我们前头的路程,仰望为我们信心创始成终的耶稣。」(来 12:1-2)
wilderness_road.wrong: 不是靠血气、也不是回望——当存心忍耐,定睛仰望。

slough_of_despond.prompt: 你陷在绝望的泥沼里。谁把你从祸坑、淤泥中拉上来,使你脚步稳当?
slough_of_despond.prompt_en: You sink in the mire of despair. Who lifts you from the miry pit and sets your feet firm?
options: 耶和华 (The LORD)
我的决心 (My own willpower)
同伴易迁 (Pliable)
运气 (Chance)
slough_of_despond.correct: 「他从祸坑里、从淤泥中把我拉上来,使我的脚立在磐石上。」(诗 40:2)
slough_of_despond.wrong: 不是决心或运气——是耶和华把人从淤泥中拉起。

wicket_gate.prompt: 窄门在前,指控如箭飞来。主应许:叩门的会怎样?
wicket_gate.prompt_en: The narrow gate is before you, accusations fly. What did the Lord promise the one who knocks?
options: 就给他开门 (It shall be opened)
须先够资格 (Must first be worthy)
门永远关闭 (The door stays shut)
要付上代价 (Must pay a price)
wicket_gate.correct: 「叩门,就给你们开门。因为凡叩门的,就给他开门。」(太 7:7-8)
wicket_gate.wrong: 窄门不凭资格或代价——只管叩,门就开。

cross_and_tomb.prompt: 在十字架前,你背上的重担滚落。这重担被谁担当了?
cross_and_tomb.prompt_en: At the cross your burden rolls away. Who bore that burden?
options: 主在木头上亲身担当 (Christ bore it on the tree)
被你自己挣脱 (I shook it off myself)
被同伴分担 (A companion shared it)
只是掉进了坟墓 (It just fell in the tomb)
cross_and_tomb.correct: 「他被挂在木头上,亲身担当了我们的罪。」(彼前 2:24)
cross_and_tomb.wrong: 重担不是你挣脱的——是主在十字架上亲身担当。

interpreter_house.prompt: 解经者用灯火照亮一间间屋子。什么是你脚前的灯、路上的光?
interpreter_house.prompt_en: The Interpreter lights each room. What is a lamp to your feet and a light to your path?
options: 神的话语 (Your word)
我的感觉 (My feelings)
世人的智慧 (The world's wisdom)
前路的运气 (Fortune ahead)
interpreter_house.correct: 「你的话是我脚前的灯,是我路上的光。」(诗 119:105)
interpreter_house.wrong: 照亮道路的不是感觉或世智——是神的话。

hill_difficulty.prompt: 陡坡难行,力气将尽。等候耶和华的人会怎样?
hill_difficulty.prompt_en: The climb is steep and strength fails. What happens to those who wait on the LORD?
options: 如鹰展翅上腾,从新得力 (Renew strength, mount up like eagles)
终必跌倒 (Will surely fall)
原地不动 (Stay where they are)
靠咖啡硬撑 (Push on by sheer grit)
hill_difficulty.correct: 「但那等候耶和华的必从新得力,他们必如鹰展翅上腾。」(赛 40:31)
hill_difficulty.wrong: 不是靠硬撑——等候耶和华的,必从新得力。

palace_beautiful.prompt: 在华美宫你领受军装,前路有恶者埋伏。你当穿戴什么以站立得住?
palace_beautiful.prompt_en: At the Palace you receive armor. What must you put on to stand?
options: 神所赐的全副军装 (The whole armor of God)
自己的功劳 (My own merit)
厚重的铠甲 (Thick steel plate)
隐身的斗篷 (A cloak of hiding)
palace_beautiful.correct: 「要穿戴神所赐的全副军装,就能抵挡魔鬼的诡计。」(弗 6:11)
palace_beautiful.wrong: 护你的不是功劳或钢甲——是神所赐的全副军装。

valley_humiliation.prompt: 亚玻伦以恐吓与控告压你。圣经说,务要怎样行,魔鬼就必离开?
valley_humiliation.prompt_en: Apollyon presses with threats and accusation. What does Scripture say to do, that the devil will flee?
options: 顺服神,抵挡魔鬼 (Submit to God, resist the devil)
与他讲和 (Make peace with him)
转身逃跑 (Turn and run)
独自硬拼 (Fight him alone by might)
valley_humiliation.correct: 「故此,你们要顺服神。务要抵挡魔鬼,魔鬼就必离开你们逃跑了。」(雅 4:7)
valley_humiliation.wrong: 不是讲和、逃跑或硬拼——顺服神、抵挡他,他必逃跑。

valley_shadow_death.prompt: 你行过死荫的幽谷,黑暗与低语环绕。你为何可以不怕遭害?
valley_shadow_death.prompt_en: You walk the valley of the shadow of death. Why need you fear no evil?
options: 因为你与我同在 (For You are with me)
因为我够勇敢 (For I am brave enough)
因为谷很短 (For the valley is short)
因为我看得见路 (For I can see the path)
valley_shadow_death.correct: 「我虽然行过死荫的幽谷,也不怕遭害,因为你与我同在。」(诗 23:4)
valley_shadow_death.wrong: 壮胆的理由不是勇气或视野——乃因祂与你同在。

vanity_fair.prompt: 集市以名利享乐相诱。圣经如何劝你待这世界与其上的情欲?
vanity_fair.prompt_en: The fair tempts with fame and pleasure. How does Scripture say to treat the world and its desires?
options: 不要爱世界 (Do not love the world)
尽情享用 (Enjoy it fully)
买一点无妨 (A little won't hurt)
随众而行 (Follow the crowd)
vanity_fair.correct: 「不要爱世界和世界上的事……这都是从世界来的。」(约一 2:15-16)
vanity_fair.wrong: 随众与纵情都是网罗——不要爱世界。

doubting_castle.prompt: 巨人绝望把你囚在牢中。你手中『应许之钥』的力量从何而来?
doubting_castle.prompt_en: Giant Despair locks you in the cell. Why can the 'key of Promise' be trusted?
options: 那应许我们的是信实的 (He who promised is faithful)
因为牢门本就松 (The door was loose anyway)
因为我力气大 (Because I am strong)
因为巨人睡着了 (Because the giant slept)
doubting_castle.correct: 「也要坚守我们所承认的指望,不至摇动,因为那应许我们的是信实的。」(来 10:23)
doubting_castle.wrong: 钥匙的能力不在牢门或你的力气——在那信实的应许者。

delectable_mountains.prompt: 牧人们在喜山接待你,指给你看远方的圣城。谁是你的牧者?
delectable_mountains.prompt_en: The shepherds welcome you and show the distant City. Who is your shepherd?
options: 耶和华 (The LORD)
我自己 (Myself)
这些牧人 (These shepherds)
前方的城 (The City ahead)
delectable_mountains.correct: 「耶和华是我的牧者,我必不至缺乏。」(诗 23:1)
delectable_mountains.wrong: 牧养你的不是人或城——耶和华才是你的牧者。

enchanted_ground.prompt: 魔境的空气催人沉睡。圣经劝我们不要睡觉,却要怎样?
enchanted_ground.prompt_en: The Enchanted air lulls you to sleep. Scripture says: not to sleep, but to do what?
options: 要警醒谨守 (Watch and be sober)
稍稍歇一会 (Rest a little while)
躺下做美梦 (Lie down and dream)
走慢一点 (Just slow down)
enchanted_ground.correct: 「所以我们不要睡觉,像别人一样,总要警醒谨守。」(帖前 5:6)
enchanted_ground.wrong: 此地最危险的是沉睡——总要警醒谨守。

river_of_death.prompt: 死河横在面前,水深可惧。你从水中经过时,有谁的应许?
river_of_death.prompt_en: The river of death lies before you. Whose promise holds as you pass through the waters?
options: 你从水中经过,我必与你同在 (I will be with you)
水会自动分开 (The waters will part on their own)
你必不沾湿 (You won't get wet)
你要自己游过 (You must swim it alone)
river_of_death.correct: 「你从水中经过,我必与你同在;你趟过江河,水必不漫过你。」(赛 43:2)
river_of_death.wrong: 凭据不是水自分或你的泳技——是祂与你同在的应许。

celestial_city.prompt: 天城的门为你而开。城里,神要为祂的子民成就什么?
celestial_city.prompt_en: The Celestial City opens to you. What will God do for His people there?
options: 擦去一切眼泪,不再有死亡悲哀 (Wipe away every tear; no more death or sorrow)
赐下金银财宝 (Give gold and riches)
让人永远沉睡 (Let them sleep forever)
论功行赏 (Reward each by his merit)
celestial_city.correct: 「神要擦去他们一切的眼泪,不再有死亡,也不再有悲哀、哭号、疼痛。」(启 21:4)
celestial_city.wrong: 天城的福不是财宝或功赏——是神亲手擦去一切眼泪。
